rulings.Case Title: VIRGINIA BALDOVIESO VS. CATHERINE BRANHAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Hearing Of Pltf.'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 12, 2008 LINE 9. PLAINTIFF'S VIRGINIA BALDOVIESO (Amended) Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication: The motion is denied. Plaintiff failed to shift the burden of proof. The Court may not consider plaintiff's supplemental separate statements; the Court may only consider the separate statement filed with the moving papers. Plaintiff failed to comply with California Rule of Court 3.1350(b). The separate statement is defective because it does not identify each cause of action, issue or affirmative defense and each piece of evidence in support thereof. Neither the causes of action not the elements are identified. Finally, plaintiff failed to address damages. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD DONG et al VS. HARRY WOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike S.L.A.P.P Suit
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 12, 2008 LINE 11. DEFENDANT HARRY WOO'S Motion To Strike S.L.A.P.P Suit: denied. Although certain paragraphs of the complaint refer to protected activity, the causes of action are premised predominantly on activity not subject to CCP 425.16. Paragraph 33 is illustrative and reflects an alleged violation of plaintiffs' rights for violation of statutes, ordinances, laws, codes and regulations. Unlawful rent increases, invasion of privacy, nuisance, breach of an oral agreement and the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing are not protected activity. The fifth cause of action focuses primarily on the protected activity of filing suit against plaintiffs and the 11th cause of action seeks, among other things, to enjoin defendant from prosecuting a pending action. Plaintiffs have presented evidence that would be sufficient to establish a basis for prevailing on their claim that unlawful detainer action was filed in bad faith and in retaliation for plaintiffs exercise of their right to seek relief from the Rent Board. As to the 11th cause of action, plaintiffs seek injunctive relief related to matters in addition to the other pending action and since there is a basis to conclude the other case was not filed in good faith, plaintiffs have demonstrated a basis for prevailing if it is assumed defendant has met his burden. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: NISDAR AZRA-AFREEN,M.D. VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 12, 2008 LINE 13. PETITIONER NISDAR AZRA-AFREEN, M.D.'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate: Hearing required. The parties are to address the following questions: (1) The persuasiveness of the respective experts, Dr. Chang and Dr. Jefferson, and the ALJ's evaluation of the weight to be given to their respective testimony; (2) How should Dr. Nisar's work be evaluated - i.e. is she being evaluated as a general family practitioner who is seeing patients in a clinic to fill prescriptions or to provide care as family practitioner or for some purpose; (3) Apart from the two experts, what testimony do the parties find to be the most persuasive; (4) The seemingly admitted poor charting made it difficult for Dr. Nisar to recall certain details or to have the chart reflect what she was saying happened, for example, the patient with the low pulse. How is Dr. Nisar's testimony and statement to the investigator to be evaluated? and (4) Why has this case proceeded so slowly and is that of any consequence in addressing the cost issue or the merits?. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: COX CALIFORNIA TELECOM LLC (U5684-C) VS. GLOBAL NAPS CALIFORNIA INC (U-6449-C)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Cox California Telecom, L.L.C.'S (U5684-C) Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 12, 2008 LINE 16. PETITIONER COX CALIFORNIA TELECOM, L.L.C.'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS ON CALENDAR, THIS DATE, FOR ORAL ARGUMENT ON THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING OF JUNE 11, 2008. THE PREVIOUSLY ISSUED TENTATIVE RULING IS AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER COX CALIFORNIA TELECOM, L.L.C. (U5684-C) Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award IS CONFIRMED. IS IT UNDISPUTED THAT THE PARTIES HAD A HEARING BEFORE THE C.P.U.C., A THIRD PARTY DECISION MAKER. THE STATEMENTS OF THE C.P.U.C ARE THE BEST EVIDENCE AS TO WHAT THE PROCEEDING WAS. THE C.P.U.C. CONSISTENTLY REFERRED TO THE PROCEEDING AS AN ARBITRATION. GIVEN THAT THE C.P.U.C. ALSO HAS ADJUDICATING AND RULE-MAKING POWERS, ITS DESCRIPTION OF HOLDING AN ARBITRATION IS PERSUASIVE AUTHORITY AS TO THE NATURE OF THE PROCEEDING. THE COURT DECLINES TO STAY THE AWARD. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 12, 2008 LINE 14. PETITIONER CYPRESS SECURITY, L.L.C.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE: The writ is denied. The Court reviews the matter under an abuse of discretion standard. The bid of Guardsmark substantially complies with the RFP. The Guardsmark bid with three alternative illustrative proposals was consistent with the RFP because the hourly rate did not change. Both Guardsmark and Cypress exceeded the page limitation and included attachments consistent with the DHS representation that was acceptable. Neither party complied with financial documentation requirement and both parties demonstrated the financial capacity to provide the services. The review panel followed the criteria in the RFP and price was only one factor. Assuming Cypress was entitled to additional points for price and/or that DHS considered higher compensation when that factor was not set forth in the RFP, DHS had the discretion to make the award where other delineated factors were favorable to Guardsmark. R&A Vending Services, Inc. v. City of Los Angeles (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 1188, 1193; Monterey Mechanical Co. v. Sacramento Regional County Sanitation District (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1391. Finally, the Board of Supervisors considered several factors including the testimony of plaintiff's representatives that explained plaintiff's position on the cost analysis. The record does not support the conclusion that price was primary factor; it was one among other factors presented to the Board. =(302/PJM/X-VC)
